interview_qa.question_text: What type of application or system do you want to build? Provide a high-level overview of the problem it solves and the value it delivers.
interview_qa.answer: I want to build a productivity and project governance application for my Business Analyst (BA) and Project Management (PM) teams. The system should standardize workflows, ensure consistency in documentation and solution design, improve tracking, control scope drift, and enable financial visibility across projects. Below is the structured end-to-end process that the application must support. 1. New Project Workflow A. Discovery & Requirement Gathering The team conducts multiple meetings to: • Gather business requirements • Understand business objectives and expected outcomes • Capture functional and non-functional requirements • Identify stakeholders and dependencies A BRD draft is prepared based on discussions and previous conversations. B. Research & Solution Validation (Before Documentation) Before drafting any formal document, the team performs structured research: • Market research • Competitor analysis • Identification of similar solutions in the market • Clear articulation of business objectives • Functional scope definition • Technical feasibility assessment: • Frontend architecture • Backend architecture • Testing strategy • Sample test data design • Infrastructure requirements The goal is to ensure clarity, feasibility, scalability, and alignment with industry best practices. C. Internal Review & Standardization After research: • The proposed solution is reviewed internally • Teams validate architecture, components, and approach • Suggestions and refinements are incorporated • Ensure consistency in: • Solution building approach • Documentation structure • Technical architecture • Estimation methodology If scope or clarity is insufficient: • Conduct structured interviews • Use AI-assisted research for validation • Obtain confirmation and formal acknowledgment from the accepting authority D. Documentation Phase Once the internal review loop is completed, the following documents are created: • Business Requirement Document (BRD) • Statement of Work (SOW) • Functional Specification Document (FSD) • Technical Documentation • Low-Level Design (LLD) • Infrastructure Architecture Document • Deployment Plan • Pre-requisites: • Functional • Frontend • Backend • Infrastructure • Test Data All documentation must follow a standardized structure across projects. E. Client Approval Documents are shared with the client for approval after internal validation. No development begins without documented client sign-off. F. Execution & SDLC Governance Once approved: • Development begins with coordination across: • Development Team • Testing Team • Design Team • Infrastructure Team • BA Team • PM Team • Scrum dashboard is created • Sprint planning is conducted • Tasks are tracked sprint-wise • Full SDLC governance is maintained 2. Existing Project Workflow For existing projects: • The process mirrors the new project workflow • Incremental requirements are received via: • Calls • Emails • Messages These are categorized as: • Within scope, or • Change Request (CR) The process remains: Research → Documentation → Internal Review → Client Approval → Execution Internal review is mandatory before sharing updates with the client. 3. Sales & Pre-Sales Workflow The team also handles sales meetings across various industries. A. Requirement Understanding • Capture client interest • Identify pain points • Understand strategic intent B. Research Before Pitch Before pitching any solution: • Market research • Competitor benchmarking • Evaluation of available market solutions • Feasibility validation C. Proposal Development Prepare: • BRD • Presentation deck • SOW Then share the refined solution proposal with the client. 4. Industry Coverage Requests are received from multiple industry verticals: • Banking • Insurance • NBFC • Mutual Funds • Other industry domains The application must support domain-specifi5. Task Allocation & Resource Planning The system must support: • Task allocation • Task prioritization • Sprint planning • Daily refinement based on team availability • Testing alignment Task planning must dynamically adjust according to: •Resource bandwidth • Dependencies • Project urgency 6. Project Monitoring & Risk Governance The application must enable: • Daily project review • Weekly progress review • Sprint-level monitoring • Risk identification and escalation • Dependency tracking (internal and external stakeholders) • Weekly progress reporting Risk and dependency visibility must be available to both internal stakeholders and clients. 7. Scope Drift & Change Request Control (Critical Requirement) The system must: • Track deviations from the original requirement • Compare development source code against approved requirements • Monitor requirements received via: • Phone calls • Emails • Messages If drift is detected: • Notify Project Manager • Notify Sales Team • Notify Department Heads • Raise it as an additional requirement • Trigger effort estimation CR tracking must be proactive, not reactive. 8. Financial Governance & Profitability Tracking The system must calculate: A. Daily & Weekly Project Cost Including: • Developer salaries • Project operational expenditure (Opex) • Capital expenditure (Capex) • Sales cost • Marketing cost • Operational execution cost B. Profitability Formula Compare total cost against Project PO value. Ensure: • Minimum 15x profit post all deductions • Highlight risk if margin falls below threshold • Identify date and action required to rectify margin The formula must provide: • Real-time financial visibility • Early risk indicators • Profitability alerts 9. Continuous Follow-Up & Completion Governance The system must enable: • Continuous follow-up on: • Pending requirements • Approvals • Dependencies • Guidance to team members on: • Pending tasks • Resolution approach • Alignment with final project objective The most critical function is ensuring clarity of end objective and disciplined execution toward it. Expected Outcome of the Productivity Application The application should: • Standardize documentation and solution building • Improve project governance • Prevent scope creep • Automate CR detection • Provide real-time financial visibility • Improve accountability • Strengthen communication • Enhance execution discipline • Protect profitability
